ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, INTERVENORS ON BEHALF OF THEIR INSURED, DEFENDANT RICH-TEX, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED. INTERVENORS FAILED TO SUSTAIN THEIR INITIAL BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO RICH-TEX. THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY INTERVENORS, SPECIFICALLY PLAINTIFF'S RESPONSES TO INTERVENORS' SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, DEMONSTRATES THAT PLAINTIFF POSSESSES, OR CAN REASONABLY OBTAIN, EVIDENCE THAT RICH-TEX MANUFACTURED PRODUCTS THAT CONTAINED ASBESTOS DURING CERTAIN PERIODS OF TIME. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION EXCERPTS THAT INTERVENORS INCLUDED ARE INSUFFICIENT TO DEMONSTRATE EITHER (1) THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT WORK WITH/AROUND RICH-TEX PRODUCTS DURING ANY POINT IN HIS CAREER OR (2) THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT WORK WITH/AROUND RICH-TEX PRODUCTS DURING THE TIME PERIOD WHEN THEY CONTAINED ASBESTOS. THE COURT CONSIDERED THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ALTHOUGH NO GROUND FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS IDENTIFIED IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION, THE GROUND WAS MADE CLEAR IN THE MOVING PAPERS. (SEE LURI V. GREENWALD (2003) 107 CAL.APP.4TH 1119, 1125-1127.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, REPORTER: CLAUDIA J. KNAP OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR 6099 (TEL: 510-451-1580)

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE FOR TRIAL THE CASES AGAINST KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC. AND HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. FORMERLY KNOWN AS KAISER CEMENT CORPORATION
Parties stipulated to have all matters heard simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to consolidate for trial the cases against Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc. formerly known as Kaiser Cement corporation, into the following subgroups: 1) wrongful death/mesothelioma; 2) living/lung cancer: 3) wrongful death/lung cancer; 4) living/other cancer; 5) wrongful death/other cancer; 6) living/asbestosis; 7) wrongful death/asbestosis; 8) living/pleural disease, is denied. The actions do not involve sufficient common questions of law or fact, economy and convenience would not be served by a joint trial, and consolidation may prejudice one or both of the defendants. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order in compliance with C.R.C. 3.1312. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Dec-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tina Yim of IMAI, TADLOCK, KEENEY & CORDERY, LLP (415-675-7000), appearing for intervenors / moving parties Hartford Fire Insurance Company, West American Insurance Company, Safeco Insurance Company Of America, Travelers Indemnity Company, Zurich American Insurance Company successor in interest to Zurich Insurance US Branch (on behalf of their insured defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.)
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Jerry Grace

RULING:
INTERVENORS ON BEHALF OF THEIR INSURED, DEFENDANT RICH-TEX, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Intervenors on behalf of defendant Rich-Tex, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Intervenors failed to sustain their initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Rich-Tex. The evidence submitted by Intervenors, specifically plaintiff's responses to Intervenors' special interrogatories, demonstrates that plaintiff possesses, or can reasonably obtain, evidence that Rich-Tex manufactured products that contained asbestos during certain periods of time. Plaintiff's deposition excerpts that Intervenors included are insufficient to demonstrate either (1) that plaintiff did not work with/around Rich-Tex products during any point in his career or (2) that plaintiff did not work with/around Rich-Tex products during the time period when they contained asbestos. The Court considered the motion for summary judgment. Although no ground for summary judgment was identified in the Notice of Motion, the ground was made clear in the moving papers. (See Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119, 1125-1127.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Claudia J. Knap of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #6099 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)

RULING:
PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL

No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Trial date is December 19, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is December 21, 2016. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all other San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 8, 2016. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than October 28, 2016. Responses to the demand are due no later than November 10, 2016. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by December 12, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on December 15, 2016. Fact discovery cut-off date is December 2, 2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is December 16, 2016. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than September 23, 2016. If plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, he must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than September 7, 2016 plaintiff must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiff's claims. No later than September 7, 2016 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on October 26, 2016 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to San Francisco Local Rules of Court Rule 5 on December 19, 2016 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by December 15, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/GLW)
